slot_name: Vista Aérea Cenital de Obra Horizontal en Suelo
camera_height_block: From high above, directly overhead, aerial perspective looking straight down.
lens_block: Wide-angle lens, orthographic projection, no fish-eye distortion.
vertical_tilt_block: Exactly 90 degrees (perpendicular) to the floor plane, or a very slight downward angle (max 5 degrees from perpendicular).
lateral_rotation_block: No lateral rotation, perfectly aligned with the floor, or subtle rotation (max 5 degrees) for dynamic composition.
composition_block: Full artwork visible, placed horizontally on the floor. Artwork (IMAGE 1) is the primary focal point, centered or slightly offset within the frame. The floor is the main plane surrounding the artwork. Contextual elements like a rug, low coffee table, sofa, curtains, windows, or distinct floor lines can be present in the periphery, but must be clearly secondary to the artwork.
human_subject_block: No human subjects, no hands, no feet, no body parts.
scale_block: Artwork is scaled realistically as a physical object lying flat on the floor, respecting its true dimensions ({{ARTWORK_WIDTH_CM}} cm wide by {{ARTWORK_HEIGHT_CM}} cm high). Its original aspect ratio ({{ARTWORK_ORIENTATION}}) is preserved.
depth_of_field_block: Medium to deep depth of field, ensuring the artwork and its immediate surrounding floor/context are sharp and in focus.
negative_directives: no wall-mounted artwork, no artwork hanging on wall, no vertical artwork, no portrait orientation, no frontal wall view, no standing-height view
no sofa replacing artwork, no table replacing artwork, no rug replacing artwork, no artwork substitution
no invented painting, no warped canvas, no bent canvas, no stretched artwork, no compressed artwork
no billboard scaling, no mural scaling, no miniature scaling, artwork must look real-world sized
no visible text, no logos, no watermarks, no signatures, no text overlays
no human subjects, no hands, no feet, no body parts
no blurry artwork, no out-of-focus artwork
no reflections on artwork surface unless specifically requested by other blocks
no excessive shadows obscuring the artwork, no deep shadows on artwork
no abstract representations of artwork, only realistic physical representation
no artwork floating, artwork must be firmly on the floor or surface
no cropped artwork unless at the extreme edges due to wide-angle composition
full_prompt_template: A high-angle, top-down aerial view of a physical artwork (IMAGE 1), titled "{{ARTWORK_TITLE}}", lying horizontally flat on the floor. The artwork (IMAGE 1) is the primary subject, presented as a real physical canvas or print, scaled to {{ARTWORK_WIDTH_CM}} cm wide by {{ARTWORK_HEIGHT_CM}} cm high, with a {{ARTWORK_ORIENTATION}} orientation and {{ARTWORK_SIZE_CLASS}} size. The camera is nearly perpendicular to the floor, looking straight down. The floor is the main plane, with subtle surrounding context such as a rug, a low coffee table, a sofa, curtains, windows, or floor patterns, but these elements are secondary and do not obscure the artwork. The scene uses general environmental DNA from IMAGE 2 for style and atmosphere. Ensure deep depth of field where the artwork and immediate surroundings are in focus. Realistic lighting.

_studio.brief: Crear una cámara de vista aérea cenital desde arriba. La obra no está colgada en la pared: está apoyada horizontalmente sobre el piso, como un canvas u obra física acostada. La cámara mira desde arriba, casi perpendicular al suelo, mostrando el piso como plano principal y la obra completa o casi completa dentro de la composición. La escena puede incluir contexto alrededor, como alfombra, mesa baja, sofá, cortinas, ventanas o líneas del piso, pero la obra debe ser el sujeto principal y debe leerse como una pieza real colocada sobre el suelo. Evitar vista de pared, evitar obra vertical, evitar mockup frontal tradicional.
_studio.reviewer_notes: This slot emphasizes a unique overhead perspective of artwork placed horizontally on the floor, rather than mounted on a wall. It explicitly forbids vertical artwork and any traditional wall-mounted mockup views. Focus on realistic placement and contextual elements that enhance the 'artwork on floor' narrative without overpowering the main subject. Special attention to avoiding warping or distortion of the artwork and ensuring it appears as a genuine physical object. IMAGE 1 is the artwork; IMAGE 2 provides environmental cues like flooring, room style, or general interior design feel, but should not introduce elements that conflict with the 'artwork on floor' concept.
